title: Configure AWS Provider with Version Constraints
description: Write a Terraform configuration with a required_providers block, version constraints, and a provider alias for a secondary region.
starterCode:
# Write a Terraform configuration that:
# 1. Sets required_version to >= 1.13.0 in the terraform block
# 2. Declares required_providers with:
#    - aws: source = hashicorp/aws, version = ~> 5.0
#    - random: source = hashicorp/random, version >= 3.5.0
# 3. Configures the default AWS provider with region = "us-east-1"
# 4. Adds an AWS provider alias 'dr' with region = "eu-west-1"

terraform {
  # Add required_version and required_providers here
}

# Default provider

# DR provider (alias)
hints: required_providers goes inside the terraform {} block
Provider alias syntax: alias = "name" inside the provider block

validationRules.errorMessage: Add required_version in the terraform block
Include aws provider with source = hashicorp/aws
Add a provider alias for the DR region
Use the pessimistic constraint operator (~>) for aws version

simulatedOutput.success:
Initializing the backend...
Initializing provider plugins...
- Finding hashicorp/aws versions matching "~> 5.0"...
- Finding hashicorp/random versions matching ">= 3.5.0"...
- Installing hashicorp/aws v5.82.0...
- Installing hashicorp/random v3.6.0...

Terraform has been successfully initialized!

Providers: aws (default: us-east-1), aws.dr (eu-west-1)
simulatedOutput.failure: Provider configuration error. Check required_providers, source paths, version constraints, and alias syntax.
